data.epi_content:
# A Secret Night Meeting with Evan

I had asked him one question.

Would this terrible cycle of returning after death ever end?

He answered as if it were obvious. If I simply died of old age without any problems, it would all be over.

When I asked the evil god about it, he said some nameless deity who enjoyed amusement had brought me here and mixed me in. When I asked why I specifically had to suffer this fate, he said I was probably just the one who caught his eye at that moment.

At first, I felt wronged, and it was disheartening to realize all my serious contemplation about why I had to endure this was meaningless—but then again, when had anything ever been meaningful? My life had been nothing but desperately clinging to survival, letting go when things got too difficult, and then continuing the cycle of suffering.

No matter how much I struggled, I remained in place, with everyone despising me terribly and trying to kill me. But now I've found someone who walks in time with me and tries to save me. I think that's enough.

After our conversation, instead of kissing on the lips, we simply pressed our lips lightly against each other's foreheads. It wasn't really a romantic atmosphere—we just wanted to escape from the stories we'd heard.

It's not that I've lost my sense of identity or certainty about myself. From the moment I opened my eyes as this young lady, I was already Erica Mecklenburg. At first, before my first death, I did deny it. But I realized long ago that trying to distinguish between us was meaningless, and at some point, I stopped the bizarre habit of talking to myself.

When I asked if accepting the magical power after offering a sacrifice would make me no longer myself, I answered that it wouldn't. That's how it is, he said.

Then was that person who had been cooped up in a room reading Vivian's love story the sacrifice?

Something felt slightly different, but I just accepted it and moved on. After all, I'm not a magician.

When everything is done, he suggested we should get married, as we had joked about before. He said he would have rings made with jewels from the Imperial Palace. Anyway, about a week has passed since our conversation by the pond.

If you asked what has changed at the Academy, I'd say everything is the same except that male students who snuck out to the alley bars have gone missing in large numbers, and some students who went to the bathroom never returned. Oh, and because of that, the atmosphere is a bit unsettled.

If you walk around the shops and streets on school grounds, everyone is talking about it.

Anyway, I don't attend classes and still stay locked in my room. I visited the specialty goods store when the Crown Prince wasn't around, bought plenty of tobacco, and have been chain-smoking.

"It should be about time for her to come."

Come to think of it, I learned a good lesson last time. Probably because I beat Lydia so loudly in the hallway, quite a lot of people came. Reflecting on that lesson, I asked Evan for sleeping powder.

Now, when Lydia comes, I'll just grab a handful of powder, throw it around, hold my breath, and that's it. After she collapses, I'll secretly drag her into my room. Evan cast a spell on my room so sound can come in but not go out. After I'm done playing with her, I'll just give her to Evan and tell him to use her as a sacrifice.

Knock. Knock.

"Miss, I was worried about you. Could you please open the door?"

Sure enough, just as I was smoking my sixth cigar, Lydia came to my door with two unfamiliar female students, just like last time.

She says she came because she was worried. Well, what can I do? I open the door and immediately throw the powder. The three of them instantly fall asleep and collapse to the floor.

It would be troublesome if someone saw me moving collapsed students in the hallway, so I quickly dragged Lydia and the two students into my room. This weak body of mine struggles even to move unconscious girls.
data.epi_content2:
"I don't even know these two. I'll just get rid of them first."

I dragged the two unknown female students to the bathtub and stomped on them until their necks bent at impossible angles. Unless their flexible spines could move to make their foreheads touch the floor—an impressive sight—I think this is quite fair treatment for vagrants who collapsed in front of someone else's room without warning. It took a while due to my lack of strength.

"Whew, that was tiring."

I'll have to ask Evan to clean this up later.

"I should also cut down on talking to myself. I've been alone for so long that it's become a hard habit to break."

Right, there wasn't a single person around me before. Now I'm fortunate to have at least one person.

I approached the peacefully sleeping Lydia, tied her hands with rope, and picked up the pistol. There's plenty of time. I'm not seeking revenge; I'm just getting rid of this insignificant girl who's been tormenting me so I won't be inconvenienced in the future. Still, my mouth was bored, so I put the smelly tobacco in my mouth instead of chocolate.

"When will she... ah, she's waking up."

Lydia rustles, trying to get up, but with her hands tied behind her back, it looks quite difficult.

"Lydia, did you sleep well?"

"Ah, Miss...? What is this suddenly...?"

Bang.

I shot Lydia in the thigh with the pistol in my hand. She must have been in quite a lot of pain, as she started rolling on the floor.

"Kyaaaaargh!!"

I got up from my seat and kicked the rolling Lydia. She tried to get up and escape with her wrists tied, but she fell backward.

"Lydia, if you don't keep quiet, I'll blow your head off right now."

When there was no answer, I put the gun to her forehead and asked again.

"Do you understand?"

She bit her lip, desperately trying to hold back her screams, and nodded frantically. But when a superior asks a question, you should answer directly. How rude to just nod.

"Lydia, when I ask you something, huh, you should answer."

After hitting her head with the gun handle, she finally answered.

"Yes, yes!"

Blood spurted from her thigh like a small fountain show and began to pool on the floor. I don't like the smell. It reminds me. Back when I was locked in that room, whenever I was beaten, that bloody smell was always in my mouth.

At least it's not my blood, which is fortunate.
data.epi_content3:
"Don't make a sound. I'll shoot as soon as you do."

I took the cigar out of my mouth and mercifully treated Lydia's wound. After all, wounds can all be treated by cauterizing them with fire. My arms and legs were treated the same way after they were cut off. Since I survived even with my limbs cut off, I suppose it counts as treatment.

Lydia made a groaning sound and shed tears, but I didn't particularly care.

She was such an easy opponent—why had I been so intimidated? She was so weak—why had I suffered so much at her hands? Pathetic.

Why did she try to torment me in the first place? Having my mouth burned, being forcibly confined in small spaces, waterboarded—I think I suffered all kinds of horrible treatment from this person. Quite creative, I must say.

Since this is a kind of closure, we should untangle the knots and clear up misunderstandings.

The way to clear up misunderstandings is simple: eliminate the problem causing them.

The simplest way to untie a tangled knot is to cut it with a knife, but I'll evolve a bit further and use the precious firearm in my hand instead of a cold weapon.

"Lydia, don't you have something to apologize for?"

"I'm sorry, I'm sorry, Miss! I must have gone crazy for a moment!"

Lydia bowed her head to me in a servile manner, as if she would lick the floor if asked. Finding her sobbing annoying, I stepped on her head that was touching the floor, applying a little pressure. Surprisingly, her crying subsided.

When you want to learn something through violence, it's important to beat someone randomly first and then make them want to talk, rather than asking questions while being violent.

I didn't even ask why she tormented me, but she's already eagerly making excuses.

"Yes, so what did you do wrong?"

"...I brought students and tried to beat you..."

Her words trail off. Well, that's because I hit her. How dare she talk about beating someone when she's trembling in fear of a single bullet.

"Not that. Tell me three other things."

Lydia started racking her brain. But how could she know? These were things she hadn't done yet, at least not in this timeline. Unable to think of anything, Lydia just kept repeating that she was sorry, trembling in fear.

It wasn't particularly pleasant. Nor did it feel especially satisfying.

Well, just as separating trash on the street might give you a slight sense of accomplishment but not exhilaration, Lydia wasn't worth pouring out stale emotions or sticky hatred. She was just that insignificant.

"Lydia, get up and walk to the bathroom."

At those words, Lydia's face showed slight despair as she limped into the bathroom. I aimed precisely at the back of her head and pulled the trigger. The bullet created a hole in Lydia's head.

One powerless, arrogant girl simply collapsed and died. The smell of the corpse can't be helped, but the blood will flow down the drain, so there's no problem. When Evan deals with it, he can just splash some hot water a few times, and the smell will disappear.

I'm glad I had a gun. There was no need to dirty anything else to kill a cockroach.
